steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: This is a text-structure question. We must map each part of the passage to a structural description.

steps.title: Reason toward the answer
steps.content: The passage (1) describes the fruit-bat finding and explains it via cost-benefit reasoning, (2) notes that most other nocturnal mammals respond similarly — marking the finding as a general pattern, then (3) introduces mongoose lemurs as an exception and explains the exception by their visual-foraging reliance.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'suggests that the other response still needs to be explained': the lemurs' opposite pattern IS explained (visual foraging changes the reward/risk balance), so the structure isn't "needs to be explained." ✗
  • Choice C — 'attributes a conflicting study result to a difference in that study's methods': the text never discusses methodology — the lemurs' difference is biological, not methodological. ✗
  • Choice D — 'an exception...that casts doubt on that explanation': the lemur exception doesn't undermine the cost-benefit explanation; it actually confirms it by showing a different cost-benefit balance still drives behavior. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
